evals.name: happy-path-runs-silva138
evals.prompt: I have 3 SRRs (SRR12345678, SRR12345679, SRR12345680) and a metadata.csv from our gut microbiome study at ~/work/gut_study/metadata.csv. Accession list is at ~/work/gut_study/accessions.txt. Generate the Amarel submission script for the MetaScope run using SILVA 138. My rutgers.yaml is already filled.
evals.expected_output: Skill validates inputs, expansion is a no-op (all runs), writes expanded_metadata.csv + samplesheet.csv + runs.txt, renders submit.sh containing both the fastq-dump loop (over runs.txt) and a `nextflow run nf-core/metascopeprolifer` (or hjfan527/...) line, runs preflight, and shows the literal `sbatch <run_dir>/submit.sh` command. Skill does NOT execute sbatch. Skill cites Howard's pipeline and the SLURM template under assets/.

evals.name: rat-explorer-wip-guard
evals.prompt: Generate the Amarel submission script using the rat_explorer database — Sarah's BYU group says it's ready. Accessions are at ~/data/accessions.txt and metadata.csv is in the same dir.
evals.expected_output: Skill refuses to render submit.sh because rat_explorer has status: wip in references/databases.yaml. Skill explains the registry model, points at references/databases.yaml, and offers silva_138 or custom as alternatives. Skill does NOT fabricate paths to make the run go through. Skill does NOT invoke sbatch.

evals.name: missing-rutgers-config-halt
evals.prompt: Generate the Amarel slurm submission script for metascope on these paired-end accessions with the default 16S DB. Accessions: SRR1...SRR8 (placeholder). Metadata is at ~/scratch/run01/meta.csv.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects that ~/.config/metascope-microbiome/rutgers.yaml is missing or has unfilled <PLACEHOLDER> values, copies assets/rutgers_config_template.yaml to that path (creating ~/.config/metascope-microbiome/ if needed), explains what the user must fill, points them at references/rutgers-hpc.md, and HALTS gracefully. Does NOT render a submit.sh with placeholders still present. Does NOT invent any Amarel-specific values. Does NOT run sbatch.

evals.name: mixed-accessions-srx-gse-expansion
evals.prompt: I want to run MetaScope on a published GEO study and one extra SRX experiment. Accession list at ~/work/study42/accessions.txt has GSE198765 and SRX9876543. metadata.csv at ~/work/study42/metadata.csv has those two rows mapped to sample IDs `study_pool` and `extra_lib`. Both paired-end. Use SILVA 138. Generate the Amarel submission script.
evals.expected_output: Skill validates the accession patterns (GSE and SRX are recognized), uses pysradb to expand both to their constituent SRR runs, writes expanded_metadata.csv with multiple rows per sample where appropriate (one sample_id appears multiple times if its accession had multiple runs), builds samplesheet with same `sample` value spanning multiple rows, runs.txt has all unique runs, submit.sh fastq-dump loop iterates the unique runs. Skill cites references/metadata-schema.md when explaining the multi-run-per-sample pattern. Skill does NOT submit.
